headline: Benzinga Asks: Did You Go To The Movies In October? What It Could Mean For Movie Theater Stocks
summary: Movie theater companies are reporting record post pandemic attendance in October. With many people opting to go to the movies for the first time in months, Benzinga took to Twitter (NYSE: TWTR) to ask its users if they were part of the record October figures.

headline: New All-Time High: Solana Becomes The Fourth Top Crypto After Overtaking Cardano
summary: Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) broke a new all-time high, overtaking Cardano (CRYPTO: ADA) and Tether's USDT stablecoin to become the fourth highest ranked cryptocurrency by market cap.

headline: Replacing A Broken Screen On An Apple iPhone 13 Will Be More Complicated And Costly Than Previous Models
summary: A new report indicates that Apple Inc's (NASDAQ: AAPL) new iPhone 13 has a significant restriction that makes it harder for consumers and independent repair shops

headline: If You Invested $100 In Shiba Inu Coin And Dogecoin On Jan. 1, 2021, Here's How Much You'd Have Now
summary: Two of the most popular cryptocurrencies center on the Shiba Inu dog and have gained loyal communities. Both coins have also seen significant increases in their valuations in 2021.

headline: Disney Rakes In $71M With 'Eternals' Opening Weekend
summary: The Walt Disney Co. (NYSE: DIS) took control of the U.S. box office this weekend with the premiere of its Marvel Cinematic Universe epic “Eternals,” which opened to $71 million in ticket sales from a release in 4,090 theaters.

headline: What Elon Musk Should Do With The Potential Proceeds From 10% Tesla Stake Sale: Twitter Has These Suggestions
Can Nio Deliver In Q3 Amid Investors Expectations For an Inflection Higher
summary: Chinese EV startup Nio, Inc.'s (NYSE: NIO) September quarter performance was mixed, at least from the deliveries perspective. As the company rolls up its sleeves to release its quarterly scorecard, here is a look at what can be expected.

headline: Warren Buffett Has Spent More On Buybacks of Berkshire Hathaway Stock Than Adding To Position In Apple
summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc (NYSE: BRK) has spent more money on buying back its own stock than adding to its position in the company’s biggest equity holding Apple Inc (NASDAQ: AAPL).
